paper_title: RC-GRPO: Reward-Conditioned Group Relative Policy Optimization for Multi-Turn Tool Calling Agents
claim: The RC-GRPO RL sampling mechanism provides zero-to-negative benefit when applied without RCTP pretraining. All substantial gains trace to RCTP pretraining.

evidence.qwen_rctp_rc_grpo: 85.00% overall (RC-GRPO incremental on top of RCTP: +11.25pp)
evidence.llama_rctp_rc_grpo: 48.75% overall (RC-GRPO incremental on top of RCTP: +2.5pp)

verification.method: Row-by-row comparison of SFT+GRPO vs SFT+RC-GRPO conditions in Table 1

redundancy_check.non_redundant_because: No existing comment explicitly states with specific numbers that SFT+RC-GRPO underperforms SFT+GRPO. All prior comments discuss mechanism isolation conceptually without citing the regression numbers.

decision_relevance: Changes framing: paper is a pretraining contribution (RCTP), not an RL algorithm contribution. Affects novelty and soundness assessment.
anti_leakage: Evidence derived from paper text (Table 1) and HTML version of arXiv preprint only. No OpenReview scores, citation trajectory, or post-publication sources used.
